title: Field Service Engineer
description: **About Us** ------------ At Thérapie Clinic, we are the fastest\-growing Aesthetic Medical Clinic in Europe. With 75 Clinics across Ireland, Northern Ireland, Great Britain and New York City and over 200 Doctors on our team we pride ourselves in being the No.1 Medical provider of Aesthetic Medical treatments. It’s an exciting time for us and we are looking for some talented people to join our team! **About the Role** ------------------ We are delighted to be recruiting for the role of a Field Service Engineer/Technician. We are looking for a person who can support with repairing, testing and installing laser systems. The candidate will provide support and training with onsite technical issues or challenges faced in clinics or by team members. We want people to ‘Do their best work at Thérapie’ and as part of the Clinic Team, you will be integral to creating that environment and culture. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ * Repair, test, calibrate and install laser systems, sub\-systems and/or sub\-assemblies * Report to supervisor any potential problems which could impact timely completion of assigned tasks, equipment shortcomings, and more complex equipment problems * Maintain complete and accurate service records * Interact with stakeholders and monitor sites * Provide on\-site technical support as required * Provide on\-site technical support for workshops and/or shows * Occasional overnight travel * Other duties and responsibilities as directed by the supervisor * Provide technical support and business guidance remotely through call center onnon\-scheduled service days. * Completion of tasks as assigned by management * Demonstrate familiarity with Laser technology and applications * Maintain a minimum of service recall * Demonstrate familiarity with Laser technology and applications **Qualifications and Skills** ----------------------------- * Working knowledge of customer needs in a medical environment * Perform product alignment and certification to specification * Adhere to company policies and accounting procedures for purchases, ordering of spare parts, van stock, and prompt return on all RMA’s * Associate's degree in electronics or equivalent experience preferred * 2\-3 years of related field service experience in troubleshooting medical electronics equipment * Technically oriented with hands\-on laser technical experience preferred * Perform product alignment and certification to specification **Over to the best part, the Perks!** ------------------------------------- * #### **Uncapped Earnings** With our uncapped commission structure, the sky's the limit! * #### **Treat Yourself** Enjoy free laser hair removal, Free EmSella and generous discounts on our skin, AWI and body treatments – because our skin maestros deserve the best. * #### **Share the Love** Avail spouse and family discounts, spreading the joy of Thérapie. * #### **Sisterly Love** 10% off Thérapie Fertility \& Optilase treatments – because family helps family. * #### **Thérapie Fertility** We offer one free treatment cycle of IVF/ICSI/RIVF to all team members after they have been with us for 2 years. * #### **Optilase Eye Clinic** We offer free laser eye surgery to all team members after they have been with us for 2 years. * #### **Skin Royalty** Discounts on leading skin brands \& treatments (Murad, Skin Theory). * #### **Career Pathways** Embark on a journey of career progression \& management training – we believe in promoting from within! * #### **Knowledge Oasis** Immerse yourself in a full week of paid training in our academy, where knowledge is power. * #### **Big Birthday Bash Leave** Enjoy an extra day off on your landmark birthdays to celebrate in style! * #### **Longevity Bonus** Earn extra annual leave days after 2 years of dedicated service. * #### **Employee Assistance Scheme** Access confidential support services for personal and professional well\-being. **Application Process** ----------------------- STEP 1 **Explore Opportunities****:** Start by finding the role that excites you. STEP 2 **Submit Your Application****:** Apply by uploading your CV. STEP 3 **Application Review****:** Our Talent Acquisition Partners will carefully review your application. STEP 4 **Initial Contact****:** If shortlisted, our Talent Acquisition Team will reach out to schedule an interview. STEP 5 **Interview Stage****:** Complete the interview process with our team. STEP 6 **Offer \& Onboarding****:** If successful, you'll receive your offer along with onboarding details. STEP 7 **Begin Your Journey****:** Attend our state\-of\-the\-art training facility to kickstart your career with us.

description: **Job Title** : Financial and Regulatory Reporting Analyst **Business Unit** : BNP Paribas Security (BP2S) Operations **Contract Type** : Permanent **Location** : Sandyford, Dublin/Parkmore, Galway (Hybrid) **Why work with us?** We are the bank for a changing world and we aim to build positive change together: * We are a team of colleagues who are enthusiastic about what we do and the impact our work has on our clients. We work in a highly collaborative, open\-minded environment that embraces innovation and creativity + We offer competitive remuneration and a comprehensive benefits package, including a discretionary bonus scheme, company pension contribution, comprehensive private healthcare packages, health and fitness subsidy, work\-from\-home subsidy, above statutory days of annual leave and numerous other benefits. + We are strongly committed to wellbeing and work\-life balance, including a 50% hybrid work model (where the role allows), paid family leave, fertility and menopause support — available to support all life stage needs. + Equality, diversity and inclusion are at the core of our culture, and we actively promote equal opportunities through creating or being part of initiatives and programmes: + Aspire Female Leadership Programme, provides development and exposure to high\-potential women in the bank, through targeted workshops, executive coaching and formal mentoring + Proud members of 100 Women in Finance and among the first signatories of Ireland’s Women in Finance charter + Joined the \#JamaisSansElles (Never Without Her) Charter, committing its executive committee to only join public or media events with panels of three or more speakers, if at least one of those speakers is a woman + Development and mobility are actively encouraged. A few of our programmes include: + Leaders for Tomorrow, a programme that fast\-tracks high\-potential talent through training, development and cross\-functional projects, preparing them for future leadership positions + Local and Global Mentoring initiatives, that pair employees with senior leaders, providing staff with platforms to gain advice on career growth topics + Ambassador Awards employee recognition scheme provides rewards to those who have embodied the Bank’s core values + Local Experts (Champions) Programme, identifying internal subject\-matter experts from specific business areas, who excel at their craft + We are a global company operating in over 60 countries with over 180,000 employees from over 150 nationalities. In Ireland we have approximately 550 employees across Dublin and Galway, working in Corporate Institutional Banking (CIB) and BNP Paribas Security Services (BP2S) and Greenval **Equality and Diversity** BNP Paribas is an equal opportunities employer and proudly cultivates a diverse workforce. We believe that diversity of experience enhances our service offering to clients and leads to a better working environment. We welcome applications from the best candidates regardless of age, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, socio\-economic background, parental and caring status, or religious belief. Our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) play a key role in promoting diversity and inclusion through initiatives, events and advocacy programmes that foster a sense of belonging for all colleagues. In BNP Paribas Ireland, we have dedicated networks for parents and caregivers, LGBTQA\+, staff from diverse cultural and religious backgrounds, alongside forums to discuss CSR and gender related topics. **Sustainable Finance** BNP continues to demonstrate the Bank’s steadfast commitment to supporting its clients in their growth ambitions by expanding market reach across assets, delivering excellence in bond markets, and showing the way in sustainable financing. **Role** * This role sits in the BNP Paribas Securities Services division (BP2S) * As a key member of the team, the objective of this role is to assist in the production of the financial and regulatory reporting obligations of the funds we administer * The core responsibilities require some subject matter expertise with attention to detail, quality control and excellent service delivery * The team works closely with the Operations functions to ensure successful delivery of financial and regulatory reporting deliverables * It is an exciting opportunity for someone with experience in fund administration and an interest in financial/regulatory reporting to be involved with a growing service within our Fund Administration business **K** **ey Responsibilities** * To assist in production of financial and regulatory reporting team obligations (Financial Reporting and Regulatory Reporting) * Ensure all financial reporting procedures are adhered to and ensure compliance with internal and external regulations * To assist in efficiently planning the financial statement preparation and audit process in conjunction with all relevant parties, ensuring that agreed timelines are achieved * Assist with the workflows for all audit engagements, act as both an internal reference and escalation point for audit issues * To keep up to date with all relevant regulatory and accounting changes and assist with re\-engineering of processes where necessary * To liaise with clients and auditors to ensure that the financial statements are audited and filed with the relevant regulatory bodies and Stock Exchanges on a timely basis * Assist with ensuring effective oversight of outsourcing vendors in accordance with Outsourcing Policy and to ensure high service levels are maintained * Preparation and review of client CBI regulatory returns (DIFR, MMIF, Monthly NAV return etc.) * Other ad hoc projects **Qualifications \& Experience Required For This Role** * Finance/Business graduate, Qualified/Part qualified Accountant (ACA, ACCA, CPA or CIMA) an advantage * Minimum of 3 years’ experience working in a financial reporting department, audit experience or fund administration roles * Understanding of accounting standards, primarily IFRS, US GAAP and FRS 102 a benefit * Be able to take responsibility and resolve issues as they arise * Ability to work to tight deadlines * Excellent organisational and Microsoft office skills * Knowledge of Fund Accounting/Investor Services or other accounting platforms an advantage * Excellent communication skills **About BNP Paribas** At BNP Paribas, we center our values on what drives the company forward. Our driving forces are: * Agility: we want to behave more simply to embrace useful innovation and digital transformation * Client Satisfaction: we believe that our success lies in being the customers’ and clients’ preferred choice. We seek to listen carefully to them and work closely with them * Compliance Culture: We believe in promoting clear rules to foster a strong culture of compliance and ethics * Openness: We promote open\-minded attitudes towards our stakeholders. We aim for everyone in Group to feel included, to have their say and be empowered **Our Strengths Are Based On Four Key Areas** * Stability: we build upon our solid, long\-term oriented management, our diversified and integrated business model and our international footprint * Expertise: We build upon our recognized and expanding knowledge of our teams * Responsibility: We build upon our culture of responsibility and integrity to ever better serve the interest of our customers * Good Place to Work: We foster a stimulating workplace where people are treated fairly and with respect **Management excellence** BNP continues to foster top\-class people management practices within our strong people focused culture. BNP Paribas managers follow the Group’s Management Charter, which emphasises a positive, empowering team culture, encouraging open communication and supporting development and wellbeing. **Compliance Culture** We believe in promoting clear rules to foster a strong culture of compliance and ethics. We would expect any incoming member of staff to respect all financial security related legislation, including anti\-money laundering (AML); countering the financing of terrorism (CFT); international financial sanctions and embargoes (IFS); anti\-bribery and corruption (ABC); KYC; ongoing client related data screening; and transaction monitoring. BNP Paribas have clear policies on these topics which all staff are expected to familiarise themselves with upon successful entry to the Group. Should you require any reasonable accommodation for the interview process, please let us know. By submitting your application, you agree to BNP Paribas completing a background check and screening in advance of any potential offer being made.

They've recently closed funding, launched their MVP, and are now working closely with early design partners while refining product\-market fit. This is a genuinely rare opportunity to join as a **Founding Engineer** and take full ownership of engineering as the product evolves. What You'll Be Doing * Taking ownership of the engineering function from day one * Working closely with the founding team to shape product and technical direction * Assessing an existing MVP and evolving / rebuilding it where needed * Designing and building a scalable, production\-ready system * Contributing to early product decisions and customer\-facing conversations * Helping define future engineering hires and team structure What They're Looking For This isn't a role for someone who just wants to manage , they need a hands\-on builder. You will need to have: * Experience operating as a Senior or Principal Engineer (Backend or Fullstack) * A track record of building and shipping real products * Experience in fintech, enterprise SaaS, or regulated environments * A solid understanding of: + system design + reliability and scalability + security and data handling Exposure to AI / LLM\-based systems is a strong plus, but you don't need to be a specialist. More important is the ability to build robust systems and apply new technologies thoughtfully. Tech (Current Stack) * TypeScript / React * Postgres / modern backend tooling * Cloud\-native infrastructure * LLM integrations (model\-agnostic approach) That said, stack is flexible. You'll have real influence over technical decisions moving forward. If you currently work with different technologies, **this is not a blocker.** Why This Role Stands Out * True Founding Engineer position with real ownership * Opportunity to shape both product and engineering culture * Strong equity offering at an early stage * Backed founders with deep domain expertise * A real\-world problem space (not just "AI for the sake of AI") * High autonomy, low bureaucracy Things to Know * Fully remote, with a preference, for candidates based in Ireland * Early\-stage environment \- things will move quickly and change often Next Steps If you're interested, apply directly or reach out for a confidential conversation. I'm happy to walk you through the company, product, and team in more detail. How to Apply To apply for this role, submit your CV via the application form provided. Alternatively, to speak in absolute confidence about this opportunity, please contact Jack Tyrrell via j.tyrrell@mcsgroup.jobs Disclaimer If you have a disability which means you require assistance at any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us directly to discuss. We are committed to providing equality of opportunity to all. Please note, we are receiving an exceptionally high number of applications at present and will be unable to shortlist candidates who are not meeting the specific requirements for this role. Due to the high volume of applications, we may be unable to provide individual feedback. We thank you in advance for your understanding.

title: From Hospitality to Consultant: 2026 - The Year You Start a Fast-Growth Career
description: * **Fast, merit\-based career growth: average performers are promoted every 6 months** * **Full training programme specially designed for people transitioning from other industries** * **Respected corporate career: climb the ranks and become a top leader and businessman** This is a **Recruitment Consultant** role at **Access** , our leading **Healthcare Recruitment** business. This role is aimed at growing our Dublin teams with ambitious, driven individuals. By specializing in recruitment, you’ll play a critical role in the current growth mission of Access, receiving **full training and mentorship** to become an expert **even if you have no prior experience in the recruitment industry.** We have several success stories from people coming from Hospitality, since these professionals are typically very resilient and hard\-working, great communicators, and often feel there’s no room for them to get promoted and grow their careers (and **we’re very big on career growth at Access** ). Take Rian. In just **24 months** , he progressed **from Associate Recruitment Executive** (our starting\-level position) **to Associate Manager** , building a high\-performing team and shaping his own leadership path. In two years, you could be his peer, and you could be telling this same story to your friends, but this time about yourself. **What Recruitment Consultants do:** * **Post job ads, review CVs, and call candidates** to assess their calibre and if they’re a good match for our clients’ hiring needs * **Guide the professionals you recruit through the onboarding process** , helping them get all the training and certificated they need in order to be able to work in the healthcare field * Once the professionals you recruit have all the bureaucracy sorted, you start **booking them on shifts across our clients.** * Every shift you book will bring you **commission on top of your basic salary** , and will bring you closer to your next promotion. **Fast Career Growth, and other Benefits:** * **Merit\-based promotions every six months** for average performers * **Clear, transparent progression** pathway from Associate to Manager * **Full training** , mentorship, and continuous upskilling * **Corporate, professional environment** with structure and accountability * **High performance recognised quickly** , not based on tenure * **Supportive team culture** with leaders who invest in your development **Compensation:** * **Competitive base salary \+ uncapped commission structure** * Average Year 1 earnings: **€45K\+** * Average Year 2 earnings: **€66K\+** * Commission paid on every shift you book \- **no clawbacks** * Due to the unlimited commission, high performers consistently earn well above these figures **What We Look For:** * 1\-3 years of experience of proven success hospitality or other fast\-paced customer\-facing roles * Strong communication skills and a professional, confident presence * Ambition to progress quickly in a structured corporate career * Resilience, accountability, and a strong work ethic **Ready to make 2026 the year your career takes off?** Don’t wait \- join Access Healthcare and start your fast\-growth journey as a Recruitment Consultant today. Contact Cesar Toscano at **cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie** or **\+353 (1\) 572 1161** to be considered.

title: General Operative / Loop General Operative
description: Become part of our SWARCO team as **General Operative / Loop General Operative** **Challenge** **About SWARCO** At SWARCO, we shape the future of transport. As part of the global SWARCO Group, we develop cutting\-edge technologies for intelligent, sustainable, and efficient mobility ecosystems worldwide. From advanced traffic management to intelligent transport systems, our work reduces congestion, enhances safety, and minimises environmental impact. Join our collaborative team, innovating for smarter, greener cities globally. What you will do We have a vacancy for a General Operative / Loop General Operative based in Dublin, reporting to the Service Delivery Manager. In this role, you will support the installation, maintenance, and repair of ITS and traffic\-related infrastructure, ensuring work is completed safely, efficiently, and to a high standard. You will work both independently and as part of a wider operational team, contributing to project delivery, fault response, and ongoing maintenance activities across customer sites. You will be responsible for carrying out loop installation and commissioning, attending faults, supporting civil works, and assisting with site surveys and inspections. The role also involves maintaining accurate documentation, ensuring compliance with health and safety procedures, and supporting colleagues, trainees, and apprentices when required. Key responsibilities include: * Installing, maintaining, and repairing ITS and CITS equipment * Carrying out loop cutting, installation, and commissioning activities * Attending Class 1, 2, and 3 faults within your training and competency level * Supporting civil works associated with installation and maintenance activities * Conducting site surveys, inspections, and quality checks * Ensuring adherence to contract requirements and KPI targets * Completing project documentation accurately and in a timely manner * Maintaining company vehicles, tools, equipment, and PPE * Supporting colleagues, trainees, and apprentices on site * Ensuring compliance with QHSSE regulations and safe working practices On a day\-to\-day basis, you will: * Carry out installation, repair, and maintenance works on ITS infrastructure * Perform loop cutting and commissioning activities * Undertake lamping and cleaning duties where required * Prepare and assemble project equipment in the workshop * Ensure PPE is worn in line with RAMS and safety requirements * Verify equipment availability and suitability prior to installation * Maintain accurate paperwork including timesheets and job records * Ensure safe working practices are followed at all times * Liaise with customers and internal teams professionally * Maintain vehicle, tools, and equipment in serviceable condition You may also: * Participate in training courses and toolbox talks * Assist with project delivery and successful switch\-on of installations * Support development of trainee operatives and apprentices **Profile** What we are looking for Essential: * Civils and construction experience * Machine/Plant operator experience * Ability to work independently * Ability to work at heights * Ability to work as part of a team * Full ‘B \& C’ driving licence * Strong safety awareness * Abrasive wheels training * Solas Safe Pass * CSCS Signing, Lighting \& Guarding at Roadworks * CSCS Location of Underground Services Desirable: * Electrical/Electronic servicing training * Understanding of ITS site installation * Experience in a similar role or within the traffic industry * Mechanical aptitude * Willingness to learn and undertake new challenges **Benefit** What we offer As well as providing a competitive salary and benefits package, SWARCO actively runs employee opinion surveys as part of our drive to provide a comfortable and supportive working environment. We will support your development, and you will also receive: * Competitive salary of €41,703 – €43,788 * Generous pension contributions * Training and development opportunities * Employee Assistance Programme * Employee discounts portal * Life assurance 25 Days holidays plus bank holidays Become a part of SWARCO. We look forward to receiving your comprehensive application via the online tool. **SWARCO Ireland Ltd \- SWARCO** Essmore House 4 Kylemore Business Park Kylemore Way Dublin 8 DO8 EWP2 Ireland *Information on processing of your personal data is available https://www.swarco.com/privacy\-information\-applicants.*

title: General Warehouse Operative / Administrator
description: Flannery Group is an Irish owned, family run business supplying catering equipment to the industry for 20 years. We have grown into one of Ireland's leading suppliers of commercial catering equipment. We are seeking a junior team member for the role of General Warehouse Operative/Administrator in a busy environment. This position will be most suitable to a junior candidate who is looking to be valued and supported in their role by an amazing team, who will also assist in our stores preparing daily shipments, processing orders, checking in deliveries, etc. This is an exciting opportunity for an enthusiastic person to join our cross\-functional team. The successful candidate will play a key role, creating an excellent impression of the organisation as well as being the first point of contact for our customers and suppliers. If you are looking for a new challenge, please give us a shout. Full training and support will be offered. **Responsibilities \& Duties** * Process quotations, sales orders and delivery documentation * Supplier order processing * Assisting with preparing of daily orders in our stores * Manage customer/supplier enquiries * Undertake any other such relevant duties as may from time to time arise and are considered appropriate **Person Specification** * Excellent oral and written communication skills * Demonstrated ability to work independently as well as part of a team * Multi\-tasking and time management skills, with the ability to prioritise tasks **Essential Requirements** * Clear understanding of what good customer service looks like * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * High level of attention to detail * Motivated to become a team key member * Dependable * Organised * Professional and hardworking * Garda Vetting may be a requirement of this post **Package** * There is a holiday entitlement of 21 days * Excellent career growth and progression * An energetic and friendly team environment * Performance bonus offered * Christmas week closure * Employee discounts * Free on\-site parking * We can offer discounted childcare rates Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €30,000\.00\-€33,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Employee discount Experience: * Administration: 2 years (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Global Collections Operations Analyst
description: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. The **Global Collections Operations Team** is a high\-precision unit based in Dublin, driving the end\-to\-end production lifecycle of negative balance accounts worldwide. We balance rigorous data integrity with automated processes to ensure every file, payment, and account update is completed flawlessly. By maintaining operational excellence, we protect the eBay brand and provide a fair, accurate, and seamless experience for our global community. Operating in a fast\-paced, team\-oriented environment, this position **reports to the Operations Manager in Dublin** . You will be responsible for the flawless execution of all daily production items, internal financial tracking, and quality assurance reviews for the eBay Marketplaces Global Collections Unit. **What You Will Accomplish...** * Production \& Reconciliation: Complete daily distribution of global vendor files, process collection restrictions, and generate outbound communications. * Vendor \& Internal Financials: Manage the internal purchase order (PO) process—including approval, funding, and monitoring—and reconcile all monthly global vendor invoices and payments. * Access \& Inventory Management: Lead all aspects of vendor access (on\-boarding, permissions, and off\-boarding), handle inventory flow through the collections' lifecycle, and serve as the liaison for internal business partners regarding agency inquiries. * Quality \& Compliance: Conduct QA reviews on collection processes, document findings for management, maintain procedure manuals, and ensure strict adherence to global policies. * Continuous Improvement: Find opportunities to harness product improvements and automation to strengthen operational controls and guarantee the seamless delivery of our collections treatment. **What You Will Bring...** The ideal candidate is a quick learner who thrives under pressure and has a vision for process improvement. You must demonstrate the ability to follow existing procedures while simultaneously looking for ways to refine them for future needs. * Experience: 3\+ years in a production\-based environment (Collections experience preferred). * Technical Savvy: Skilled in all MS Office tools (Excel, Word, PowerPoint); experience working with remote groups is essential. * Communication: Excellent written and verbal skills demonstrating skill in effectively planning and coordinating business needs. * Agility: Ability to work effectively amid tight deadlines and deliver on time. You must be flexible with your schedule as production delivery determines business needs, including periodic on\-call/off\-hours duties. * Customer Focus: A strong understanding of operational realities paired with an outstanding dedication to the customer experience. **Additional Details** eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer (Commissioning)
description: **Commissioning Engineer – Dublin** (Nationwide Travel Required) Design Build Search is currently seeking a **Commissioning Engineer** based in the Dublin/Meath/Kildare area to join our clients growing team. The company delivers projects nationwide, and the successful candidate will be comfortable with regular travel across Ireland as required. This is a fantastic opportunity for a recent electrical engineer graduate looking to become part of an established, close\-knit team with a strong reputation for delivering high\-quality projects. **Responsibilities** * Commission smoke and natural ventilation systems in accordance with project specifications and EN12101 standards. * Interface with BMS systems via Modbus/BACnet gateways. * Configure and test D\+H control panels (CPS\-M), actuators (e.g., CDC series), and accessories. * Perform site acceptance tests and final handover inspections with clients and consultants. * Produce commissioning reports, operation manuals, and compliance documentation. * Collaborate with project managers and installation teams to ensure timely delivery. * Assist in fault finding and on\-site technical support as required. **Requirements** * Electrical Engineering qualification (Level 6\+ or equivalent). * 1 year of experience commissioning ventilation or building automation systems is desired * Strong understanding of electrical schematics and low\-voltage systems (24V DC). * Experience with control panels, smoke ventilation, or natural ventilation systems preferred. * Familiarity with EN12101\-2 / EN12101\-10 compliance is an advantage. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Full clean driving licence if you’re interested in learning more, apply with your CV today for a confidential discussion.

title: Graduate Management Trainee - Dublin
description: **Overview** We’re Enterprise Mobility. A family\-owned, global mobility leader with a $39 billion turnover, nearly 90,000 team members, and operations in 95 countries. Led by CEO Chrissy Taylor, the third generation of the Taylor family, we’re built on a legacy that gives us the stability to focus on the long\-term success of our people, our customers and our business. **Why join the Graduate Management Training Programme?** As a Graduate Management Trainee, you'll have the freedom and support to explore your leadership potential \- and the opportunity to become a branch manager in one of our retail operations in as little as two years. In fact, most of our senior leaders began their careers in this very role \- including our current CEO. From day one, we’ll invest in you. You’ll be in a supportive environment where you’ll take on real responsibilities and gain invaluable hands\-on experience in customer service, sales, marketing, finance, operations, and more. **We work hard and reward hard work** Your work will be recognised with performance\-based incentives and opportunities for continued promotion. Our unique promote\-from\-within culture means you can keep your career moving forward without having to change organisations. **Award\-winning training and development** Whether you’re building on existing strengths or developing new ones, your growth is our priority. Through classroom learning, on\-the\-job training, and mentorship, you’ll have the tools and support to take the next step – and the one after that. **Our doors are open** As a Graduate Management Trainee, your experience at Enterprise Mobility will be as unique as you because you shape who we are as much as we shape your skills, path and potential. We pride ourselves on opening our doors to a variety of voices, giving way to an experience that’s both inclusive and that inspires diversity of thought. **Responsibilities** From your very first day, you’ll be trusted with real responsibility and exposed to all areas of our business. You’ll develop skills in: **Customer Service:** deliver exceptional experiences by confidently handling reservations, resolving enquiries, and building rapport with a diverse range of customers. **Sales and Marketing:** connect with local business partners, grow your network and develop lasting relationships. **Financial Control and Profitability:** understand the financial mechanics of a successful business, including cost control and interpreting profit and loss statements. **Operations and Logistics:** learn how to manage a fleet, plan strategically, and drive performance. **Leadership and Development:** take the lead in mentoring, training, and developing your own team \- with the opportunity to manage and promote others as you grow. **Qualifications** * A Bachelor’s degree is preferred; however, professional experience can be substituted if applicable. * You must have a full manual Republic of Ireland, UK or EU driving licence, but we do make accommodations for applicants who don’t drive due to a disability. * No drug or alcohol related offence on driving record within the last five years is permitted. **Additional Information** * In accordance with current employment permit regulations, we are unable to support an employment permit application for this role, as it is not eligible under the current system. * Regardless of your socio\-economic background, university attended, subject studied, and degree attained, we will always look at how you perform against our competencies and will judge you on that alone. * Please let us know about any accommodations you may need to participate in our recruitment process. * Please limit your application to only one job posting based on where you live and/or plan to work. Applying to multiple locations will delay your application being processed. * This job posting is for applications within the following location(s): Dublin

title: Graduate Surveyor (Cork)
description: **Who Are We** Founded in 1896, Knight Frank was registered as Knight Frank LLP a Limited Liability Partnership, on 3 November 2003, registered in England \& Wales at 55 Baker Street, London, W1U 8AN with the registered number of OC305934\. Headquartered in London, UK, Knight Frank is one of the world's leading independent real estate consultancies. We work responsibly in partnership to enhance people's lives and environments ***in over 50 Markets, 600\+ Offices, 20,000\+ People, 1 Global Network.*** At the heart of all we do are our Clients. A relationship built on trust is vital. As trusted partners in property, we act with integrity and care, understanding our clients’ unique needs and consistently thinking about the bigger picture to provide personalised, clear and considered advice across all areas of property. Through our deep understanding of key markets and sectors – both emerging and established – we are dedicated to meeting and exceeding their property goals. A true partnership is a balanced one, and our People are passionate about, and committed to, making our business inclusive and diverse. We give every individual the space and opportunity to perform at their best and be recognised for the immense value they bring. We empower everyone to have autonomy in their role and encourage them to use their voice in how we can make a positive impact as a firm and shape the future of real estate, the built environment and the impact on the communities we serve. Our collaborative and engaged teams provide excellent and dedicated client service. In our workplace, opinions are respected, everyone is invited to contribute to the success of our business and innovation, and new ideas are celebrated. In 2021 we celebrated our 125th anniversary and set out Our Desired Future, cemented in three pillars: People Potential, Client Centricity and always Creating our Future, together. Learn more about what sets us apart here. **About The Role** Knight Frank is seeking a motivated and ambitious Graduate Surveyor to join our Commercial team in Cork. This role offers an excellent opportunity to develop your career within a dynamic and supportive environment. You will gain exposure to a broad range of commercial property activities while working alongside experienced professionals. Full support, guidance, and resources will be provided to help you successfully pursue and achieve your APC qualification. **Responsibilities** * Assist with valuation instructions and inspections for all property sectors including investment, development land, office, industrial and retail for the purposes of secured lending and fund valuations. * Contribute to the delivery of all projects to include inspections, surveys, researching, drafting reports and valuations, inputting comparable information into our database. * Assist with agency instructions to include the sale and leasing of all types of commercial property. * Assist with the marketing of properties and pitches. * Assist with lease advisory instructions to include rent reviews and lease renewals. * Maintain and update databases, ensure timely responses and that all details are recorded accurately on the relevant system. * Utilise all relevant technology systems provided, maintain up to date and accurate records, ensuring valuations are continually and appropriately recorded. * Collaborate with the wider Commercial team, contributing to the delivery of the business plan, while supporting each other in the achievement of KPIs. * Identify and act on referrals as they present themselves across Knight Frank. * Live the Company Values in all dealings with colleagues and take responsibility for own development by undertaking activities to broaden experience and market knowledge, while also sharing your expertise and providing coaching and feedback to others. * Support the enhancement of the Knight Frank brand. * Develop client relationships and seek to build on our client base. **Requirements** * SCSI accredited degree. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, ability to multitask and meet deadlines. * Proficient in MS packages. Knowledge of relevant IT packages such as Argus would be an advantage. * Excellent report writing and numeracy skills. * PSRA Licence. * Full clean driver licence. Competitive salary Please note: this is a Direct Search led by Knight Frank. Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted nor will fees be paid for unsolicited CVs, even if provided by PSL agencies.

title: Human Resources (Learning and Development) Internship
description: **Job Description** **Fidelity Investments Human Resources (Learning and Development) Internship** **Location: Dublin** **Placement Dates: June 2nd – August 21st, 2026** The Intern Program with Fidelity Investments is a comprehensive programme designed to give students an introduction to a professional global environment. starters and leavers processes, including induction, payroll related paperwork and system updates. * Preparation of HR documentation including contracts of employment, changes to terms and conditions of employment and performance appraisals. * Regular data input into the HR database /recruitment systems, generating HR reports and conducting analysis as required and ensuring compliance with Working as part of a busy HR team, supporting other HR team members as necessary. * Providing general administrative support including diary management, scheduling of meetings, room bookings and occasional event management. * Other HR Administrative tasks and ad hoc projects as requested. **Requirements:** * Degree preferably in HR / Business (or similar) **and/or** * CIPD qualification * A minimum of 1 year HR administration experience, ideally within professional services. * Excellent attention to detail, numerical skills and problem\-solving abilities. * Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to communicate effectively in a professional manner with all levels of the business. * A high degree of discretion and the ability to work with sensitive and confidential data. * An interest and understanding of Irish employment law and HR practices. * Strong Microsoft office skills. Experience in working with HR systems, an advantage. * Good time management skills, the ability to manage a busy workload and meet required deadlines. * A positive attitude, the ability to take ownership and responsibility for the completion of tasks and the ability to take and demonstrate initiative, as appropriate. **Our Offer** Competitive salary Pension contributions Life assurance and income protection cover A great working environment A lively sports and social club **Applications** To apply for this role, please email careers@mccannfitzgerald.com with your CV and, for a confidential discussion or for more information, please contact Eimear Power (Recruitment Manager) on \+353 1 607 12 92

title: In-House Commercial Lawyer
description: Barden is working with a well known business in the **infrastructures/energy sector** seeking a solicitor with strong commercial contracts experience to join its legal team. This is an organisation reputed for offering excellent working environment, work\-life balance and flexibility. The company operates a hybrid model with **2 days onsite in the office** . **THE ROLE** The successful individual will work on a variety of **procurement contracts** , primarily those related to goods and services (construction, IT services, etc), as well as other **supply chain matters** . The position will initially be offered as a fixed term contract (12 months), however it's common for this business to retain employees on fixed term contracts and for opportunities to arise within the legal team. *Full job spec is available.* **THE PERSON** * Qualified Solicitor in Ireland; * A minimum of 2 years' PQE providing legal advice on commercial issues and reviewing and negotiating commercial contracts; * Solid commercial legal training, ideally with a top tier law firm; * Experience in energy/infrastructures, construction, or procurement contracts/legislation is an advantage but not a requirement.

title: Infrastructure Credit – Core Portfolio Management - Associate -  Dublin
description: ****About This Role**** **Associate – Core Portfolio Management** **Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP), a part of BlackRock** ****About This Role:**** The Infra Credit platform spans all major infrastructure markets and sectors across the capital structure with $30\.7bn of assets under management across Investment grade and non\-investment grade strategies. The team has deployed nearly $29\.0 bn of capital across 270\+ assets. The GIP team are highly experienced with extensive expertise in originating, structuring and monitoring of Infrastructure investments which generate attractive risk adjusted returns with a focus on capital preservation and income enhancement. This is an exciting opportunity for a highly motivated and experienced professional to join the Core Portfolio Management Infrastructure Debt team supporting GIPs infrastructure credit business globally. The successful candidate will work closely with an experienced team in a collaborative and friendly environment. The role serves as a key liaison between internal teams, external partners and stakeholders, driving operational excellence and supporting the firm’s growth and performance objectives. This is a **hands‑on operational role** with exposure across the full investment lifecycle, from allocation assessment, deal onboarding and investment execution through to valuations, reporting, and ongoing fund and client support as well as opportunities to participate in fundraising processes. The Associate will play a key role in driving investment execution, maintaining strong controls and scalable processes as well as having opportunities to enhance the platforms data capabilities as GIP continues to grow and launch new products. ****Key Responsibilities:**** **Investment Process \& Valuations** * Co\-ordinate review and feedback on investment allocations and confirm adherence to Allocation Policy (e.g. investment guideline compliance, portfolio construction targets, etc.). * Complete pre\-trade compliance checks across all portfolios and mandates to confirm potential investment does not breach any agreed terms or investment guidelines * Anticipate and resolve operational issues arising during the evolving investment processes (e.g., trade execution, settlement, portfolio compliance, hedging, etc.) * Oversight of settlement day funding with our GATS/settlements team ensuring alignment with regards to settlement mechanics and investor nuances. * Work with external counterparties, namely, borrowers, advisors, sponsors to request specified documents; ensure they are provided in a timely and accurate manner * Integrate newly acquired portfolio assets ensuring accurate cusip set up and recording in Aladdin. * Assist in the co\-ordination of the monthly and quarterly valuation process, working with investment teams, valuation agent and pricing teams. * Assist in the review of monthly/quarterly valuation outputs to ensure reasonable questioning large variance month over month. * Develop and maintain relationships with key BlackRock/GIP operational professionals (e.g., legal, tax, trading operations, portfolio compliance, pricing, cash ops, bank loans, derivative operations and fund administration). * Deal management / reporting * Review monthly and quarterly client reporting ensuring accuracy and consistency and providing feedback to ACP team accordingly. * Ensure full and timely compliance with relevant regulatory reporting. * Assist in the provision of management information to senior management stakeholders * Enhance reporting quality and efficiency by improving Power BI dashboards and reducing manual workloads across the platform. * Assist in the preparation and presentation of board reports * Apply AI to streamline reporting and documentation i.e. for drafting of routine investor reports, extraction of key data from documentation to speed up processes etc. * Support responses to ad\-hoc client and internal data requests including investor specific reporting, liquidity analysis and operational queries. * Assist in reviewing operational implementation of side letters and client specific report obligations. * Contribute to the smooth operational launch of new strategies and accounts partnering closely with senior core pm team members. **Cash management** * Monitor liquidity for all private funds, including cash forecasting, borrowing capability, and dry powder. * Co\-ordination of investor capital calls and finance drawdowns to ensure funding requirements are met. * Co\-ordination of distributions from fund to investors. * Oversee the payables process where necessary. * Oversee the ongoing management of subscription lines and leverage facilities to ensure they are used optimally. * Partner with GATS, NAVO, asset management and investment team to ensure correct booking of interest payments, repayments of capital, and fee collection from underlying investments and through portfolio/fund structure **Process Improvement \& Controls** * Identify opportunities to improve, standardize and automate operational processes helping reduce manual effort and operational risk, considering AI to assist where appropriate. * Contribute to the documentation of procedures, controls and best practices as the platform continues to scale. * Participate in cross\-team initiatives aimed at strengthening Core PM’s operating model and integration within the broader BlackRock/GIP ecosystem. ****Development \& Growth Opportunities:**** **This role offers strong long\-term development potential within GIP, a part of BlackRock:** * End\-to\-end exposure to complex infrastructure investment structures, including leveraged and unlevered funds, SMAs, rated note structures and bespoke client solutions. * Hands‑on experience across portfolio operations, valuations, reporting, and client interaction, providing a deep understanding of the investment lifecycle. * Opportunities to contribute to process design, automation initiatives and platform build‑out, particularly as new funds and accounts launch. * Opportunity to join a high\-profile, fast\-growing and dynamic business that is a key strategic priority for the Firm. * Grow with a friendly entrepreneurial team at the center of the firm’s Private Debt business. * Significant exposure to globally oriented, prestige projects in a fast\-growing sector. * Strong brand in the Private Debt Industry ****Candidate Profile (indicative):**** * Ideally qualified or part qualified accountant. * Bachelor’s or master’s degree in finance, accounting, business management or * related discipline (or equivalent experience). * Strong attention to detail, organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment. * Self\-motivated with ability to operate with modest oversight. * Comfortable working across teams and with multiple stakeholders. * Proactive mindset with an interest in improving processes and building scalable solutions. * Strong financial modeling skills and proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including knowledge of advanced functions. * Interest in infrastructure investments and complex fund structures. * Experience in Python, SQL, Aladdin Software and Power BI will be viewed positively, but not required. ****Our Benefits**** To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of employee benefits including: retirement investment and tools designed to help you in building a sound financial future; access to education reimbursement; comprehensive resources to support your physical health and emotional well\-being; family support programs; and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. **Our hybrid work model** BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock. ****About BlackRock**** At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress. This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive. For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock \| Twitter: @blackrock \| LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected characteristics at law.

title: Insurance Operations Analyst-12 month Fixed Term Contract
description: **Introduction** Welcome to Gallagher \- a global community of people who bring bold ideas, deep expertise, and a shared commitment to doing what’s right. We help clients navigate complexity with confidence by empowering businesses, communities, and individuals to thrive. At Gallagher, you’ll find more than a job; you’ll find a culture built on trust, driven by collaboration, and sustained by the belief that we’re better together. Whether you join us in a client\-facing role or as part of our brokerage division, our benefits and HR consulting division, or our corporate team, you’ll have the opportunity to grow your career, make an impact, and be part of something bigger. Experience a workplace where you’re encouraged to be yourself, supported to succeed, and inspired to keep learning. That’s what it means to live The Gallagher Way. **Overview** We are looking for an Operations Support Analyst to join our Mergers \& Acquisition team and to play a key role in assisting the Operations Manager and Operations Director in the execution of integration plans and operational alignment of our a newly aquired brokerage into Gallagher Ireland. You will provide essential administrative and operational support, ensuring compliance, efficiency, and the successful delivery of M\&A\-related activities. This role is based from our Dublin office and a small amount of travel as and when required will be expected to other office location in Ireland. We offer hybrid working and expectations for this role to be office based two to three days a week. **How You'll Make An Impact** * Assist in the execution of integration plans for newly acquired insurance businesses, supporting operational alignment across functions. * Provide administrative support for all M\&A\-related activities, including system upgrades and operational changes. * Provide day\-to\-day operational support to the Operations Manager and Operations Director. * Assist in maintaining accurate data and documentation, ensuring compliance with regulatory and internal standards. * Support process harmonisation, improvements, and automation initiatives to streamline workflows and reduce operational bottlenecks. * Coordinate with internal teams (Finance, Compliance, IT, Trading Teams) to resolve operational issues promptly. * Monitor and report on integration KPIs and operational performance metrics, highlighting areas for improvement. * Assist in system updates and data migrations, working closely with IT and business teams to minimise disruption. * Support risk and compliance activities, including audits, regulatory reporting, and adherence to governance frameworks. * Act as a point of contact for operational queries, delivering high\-quality service to internal stakeholders and external partners. **About You** * Previous experience in an administrative or operational support role, preferably within the insurance or financial services sector. * Excellent organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities effectively. * Excellent attention to detail and a commitment to maintaining high standards of accuracy. * Fantastic communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate across teams and build relationships with stakeholders. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and familiarity with data management systems. * A proactive and problem\-solving mindset, with a focus on continuous improvement. * Knowledge of risk, compliance, and regulatory frameworks is desirable but not essential.

title: Intern, Software Engineer
description: **Job Requisition ID \#** 25WD92004 **Position Overview** At Autodesk, we’re reimagining what’s possible. As a global leader in 3D design, engineering, and entertainment software, we help people everywhere imagine, design, and create a better world. Right now, we’re transforming how our products work by moving to the cloud—opening up exciting opportunities for collaboration, innovation, and impact. What sets us apart isn’t just our technology, but our culture. At Autodesk, you’ll find an inclusive, supportive environment where early\-career talent can grow, be creative, and make real impact. It’s one of the reasons we’ve been consistently recognized by **Fortune, Forbes, and Glassdoor as a top place to work** . Over three months in our Summer 2026 Internship Program, you’ll gain hands\-on experience, mentorship, and opportunities to grow your engineering and domain expertise—while making real contributions to Autodesk’s design, manufacturing and construction technologies. Alongside technical growth, you’ll develop soft skills and build connections with other interns—locally in your office, regionally across EMEA, and globally—through programs designed just for interns. You’ll work with a team of Developer Advocates experts in a dynamic, supportive environment, collaborating on the definition and development of core technologies. Along the way, you’ll gain exposure to our exciting tech stack, which includes modelling, Design Data, AI \& DevOps tools. You will be working closely with the engineering team developing the core software building blocks on which current and future Autodesk products are being built. We are looking for someone who is enthusiastic about learning new technologies and to promote technologies to Autodesk partners. You are able to face new technologies and challenges and work in a dynamic and collaborative environment with some very passionate and awesome people. If you’re an innovative and fun team player, who strives to learn and improve, you’ll fit right in. We are a global team, and you will collaborate with a variety of people and teams from around the world. Take an active role in writing code samples, work on proof of concept using rapid prototyping principles and present technology in various events. Produce high quality readable code, covered with tests, and participate in code reviews. Continuously learn and improve your coding and design skills. **Several potential projects are available,** and we’ll assign you to the one that best aligns with your **interests and skills** , ensuring the role is tailored to you while giving you meaningful, impactful work. **Responsibilities** * Prototype, implement, and maintain production software components and cloud services. * Collaborate with experienced engineers to design, plan, develop, refactor, test, deliver, and maintain complex features across desktop, cloud and web components. * Participate actively in Agile development using LEAN methodology as a member of a scrum team. * Gain hands\-on experience with commercial development processes used at Autodesk. * Collaborate with engineering mentors to define project scope and milestones. * Present progress and final outcomes to internal stakeholders. **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently enrolled as an active student in a degree program. * Predicted First or Upper Second\-Class degree in STEM Field (e.g., Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, Physics) * Please include your estimated graduation year and month in your application. * Familiarity with AI/ML concepts, APIs, and agent frameworks. * Experience with databases, REST/JSON\-RPC APIs, or web development is a plus. * Passion for developer tools, open\-source, and AI innovation. **The Ideal Candidate** * Interest in design, 3D modelling or geometry, and technology. * Experience in any programming language such as .NET, and/or Typescript. * Experience in Python for AI. Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made. We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity\-and\-belonging

title: Inventory & Asset Associate
description: **Job Title: Inventory \& Asset Associate** **Location:** Dublin **Job Type:** 6\-Month Contract (with possibility to extend) **Working Pattern:** Monday to Friday, 40 hours per week **Overview** We at Gempool are currently seeking **Inventory \& Asset Associates** to support operations within a large\-scale warehouse environment in Dublin. This role is suited to physically fit individuals with strong logistics or general operative experience, who are comfortable working with heavy equipment and managing inventory movement in a fast\-paced environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Support day\-to\-day inventory and asset management activities * Move, lift, and organise large racks and equipment safely * Assist with receiving, storing, and relocating assets within the warehouse * Conduct basic inventory checks and maintain accurate records * Ensure equipment and materials are handled in line with site procedures * Work collaboratively with warehouse and logistics teams * Follow all health \& safety guidelines at all times **Requirements** * Minimum 2 years’ experience in logistics, warehousing, or general operative roles * Physically fit and comfortable with manual handling and moving heavy equipment * Experience working in a large warehouse or operational environment * Strong attention to detail and reliability * Ability to follow instructions and work as part of a team * Good understanding of health \& safety practices

title: Investment Performance and Risk Analyst
description: * Full Time Permanent position * Hybrid role based in our City Centre offices **What we offer** We have embraced a hybrid working model for most of our positions, which means that you can enjoy a balanced approach of working from home for part of the week and working from the office for the remainder of the week. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including competitive salaries and bonuses, robust Learning and Development support, excellent Defined Contribution pension and comprehensive Wellbeing initiatives and support to name but a few. Further details on our benefits package can be accessed here Benefits (life\-careers.com) At Irish Life, our purpose is to help people build better futures. Our company values shape everything we do and underpin our vision of being the driving force behind Ireland’s financial, physical, and mental well\-being. We believe in doing the right thing—acting with integrity and making fair, responsible decisions. We aim high, setting ambitious goals and constantly find better ways to innovate and improve. Our customers are at the heart of everything we do, and we are dedicated to understanding their needs and exceeding their expectations. We believe that as a team we work best together, in a supportive, collaborative environment where every idea and contribution is valued. **Role Overview** As part of the Keyridge Performance and Risk Analytics Team, you'll play a crucial role in measuring and analysing the performance and risk of investment strategies crafted by Irish Life Investment Managers (ILIM), Setanta Asset Managers (Setanta) and Keyridge UK. Picture yourself delving into the intricacies of investment returns and investment risk, deciphering the impact of each driver on managed portfolios, and collaborating closely with fund managers and client\-facing teams. The Performance and Risk Analytics Team is constantly looking to improve on our capabilities. Your expertise will contribute to the redesign of work practices, testing of enhanced solutions, and shaping the future of investment analysis. **What you will help us to achieve** * Produce performance measurement \& investment risk analysis for ILIM, Setanta and Keyridge UK\-managed funds that meet our high service standards. * Innovate by identifying and implementing process improvements, automation, and risk reduction techniques to expand the function's capabilities. * Monitoring and reporting on fund risk and performance, in both the absolute and benchmark relative senses and analyse the drivers for any change. * Contribute to crafting new reporting and analysis for onboarding new clients, funds, and services. * Cultivate relationships with internal clients, ensuring smooth collaboration on analysis and timely approval of regular performance and risk reporting, including reporting of the ESG performance and risks in the portfolios. Deliver swift and effective responses to team queries, identifying trends and opportunities for process enhancement. * Collaborate closely with teammates to fulfil shared responsibilities. * Embrace new challenges as assigned by the Team Manager. **What we are looking for** If you're well\-versed in investment asset classes, return \& risk characteristics, and investment strategies, your knowledge will be a valuable asset. (If you don’t have this level of knowledge and experience, that’s ok – we will provide the training and support to help set you up for success). The ideal candidate will wield exceptional problem\-solving skills, a knack for diagnostic insights, and the ability to distil complex information into a clear, concise format for team stakeholders. **What you will need to be successful in the role** * A degree (or higher) in a Business/Finance or numerical field (e.g., Maths, Economics, Statistics, Accounting, Actuarial, Finance, Engineering or Science). * A CFA qualification, progress towards it, or interest in pursuing is advantageous. * Prior experience in an analyst role is beneficial, with performance analysis or risk management experience being a plus. * Demonstrated awareness of financial markets and asset classes, along with knowledge of trading/portfolio operations * Demonstrable experience in investment risk management, including identifying, measuring, and monitoring key portfolio risks (e.g. market, credit, liquidity, and concentration risk) and articulating their impact to stakeholders. * Proficiency in advanced Excel, PowerQuery, and SQL. * Remarkable organisational skills with a track record in demanding environments. * Familiarity with Performance Measurement concepts, attribution models, and performance or risk systems like SimCorp Dimension, Factset PA, MSCI RiskMetrics or BarraOne would be considered an advantage. **About us** Keyridge Asset Management Limited, the investment management company within Irish Life Assurance, part of Great\-West Lifeco (GWL) Group, manages assets of over €150bn. Keyridge provides investment products and solutions to both institutional and retail clients, offering a full range of active and indexed funds together with tailored investment solutions meeting specific clients objectives. The company reserves the right to draw up a shortlist as part of the selection process. Where Agency assistance is required the Irish Life Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Irish Life by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role. Irish Life is proud to be an Equal Opportunities employer. We have created an environment of inclusion and growth to help people achieve their potential, to ensure they can bring their whole selves to work and to feel valued. We celebrate diversity of thought and perspective and want all of our employees to feel valued, respected and supported. We have built a culture that promotes equality and inclusion for all of our current and future employees and strive to ensure that our behaviours, processes and practices are aligned to this culture. If you require any accommodations during the recruitment process please contact lifecareers@irishlife.ie and we will be delighted to ensure you are fully supported to be your best. Keyridge Asset Management Limited, trading as Irish Life Investment Managers and trading as Setanta Asset Management, supports Equal Opportunity and is authorised and regulated as an investment firm by the Central Bank of Ireland.

title: Junior Contract Administrator
description: Turner \& Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries. Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success. Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions. We are majority\-owned by CBRE Group, Inc., the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with our partners holding a significant minority interest. Turner \& Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier programme, project and cost management offering in markets around the world. Please visit our website: www.turnerandtownsend.com **Job Description** The Junior Contract Administrator is responsible for providing support to the Commercial Administration Lead regarding the management and administration of contracts within the client organisation. The role holder is responsible for the day\-to\-day administrative tasks related to contract management, including drafting, reviewing and managing contracts. The Junior Contract Administrator ensures all contracts comply with legal and organisational guidelines. The role holder prepares and organises contract related documents, maintains record and supports contract negotiations. **Principal Accountabilities** * Assist in drafting, reviewing, and finalising of all contracts, ensuring that all terms and conditions are clear and comply with the client organisations polices and legal requirements. * Maintain organised and up\-to\-date records of all contracts and related documents, ensuring that all contract documentation is stored securely and is easily accessible. * Identify, assess, and mitigate risks associated with contract administration. * Serve as a point of contact for internal and external stakeholders regarding contract\-related inquiries. * Update and maintain the contract management system with accurate and current information. * Communicate contract terms and conditions to internal and external parties. * Track contract milestones, deadlines, and renewals. * Generate reports on contract status and performance, providing support during contract negotiations. * Manage contract\-related data, including contract management software or databases. * Assist with invoicing, payment processing, and other financial tasks related to contracts. * Prepare reports on contract status, performance, and compliance. * Support communication and collaboration with vendors and suppliers. * Perform additional tasks, activities or other duties as may be required or assigned as part of the role. **Experience** * 3 years’ experience in a contract administration support role in a high value rail projects or projects of a similar scale / complexity. * Experience in assisting with the drafting, reviewing, and administration of commercial contracts. * Experience dealing with a range of stakeholders at all levels across the organisation. **Skills** * Good influencing skills to manage and motivate teams in support of delivery of high risk, value and complex project and programmes. * Ability to coordinate and organise multiple sources of reporting inputs / performance data and deliver project / programme reports as required. * An ability to work effectively in a team environment. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills to facilitate clear and consistent dialogue between all parties involved. * Ability to govern and drive outcomes to ensure high quality service. * Comprehensive knowledge of cost management principles, methods, techniques and tools, including the standard method of measurement. **Qualifications** * C Eng / BEng / BA / BSc cost management, quantity surveying, estimating management or in a related discipline. **Additional Information** **What we offer you:** * Full time * Competitive remuneration and attractive range of benefits * 8% Pension * 23 days Annual leave (\+ 2 company days) * Opportunity to work on impactful and innovative projects * Career development opportunities both in Ireland and globally * Opportunity to work with a diverse group of talented and collaborative colleagues Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change. We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work\-life balance. Turner \& Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community. Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner \& Townsend and our exciting future projects: Twitter Instagram LinkedIn
