title: Junior QS
description: *Please note: This job requires you to have Irish/UK experience and can not offer any sponsorship* **Job Summary:** Our client is offering an excellent opportunity for a Quantity Surveyor to join a growing business with a clear strategic plan for expansion. Reporting to the Commercial Director and based in East Meath on a hybrid basis, this role will suit someone looking to progress their career in a company with strong long\-term ambition. **Role Description:** * Manage all aspects of quantity surveying for construction projects. * Develop the supply chain. * Oversee project procurement. * Prepare monthly project payment applications and cost reports. * Monitor project costs and agree variations. * Remeasure site works. * Report monthly to senior management on financial performance. * Prepare and present cost and risk analysis. * Manage all aspects of tendering, including financial assessments. * Ensure best value is achieved across all elements. * Audit and report on subcontractor and supplier returns. **Suitable Candidates Should Have:** * Residential experience, with modular or timber frame experience preferred. * A minimum of 2 years’ experience in a similar role. * A third\-level qualification in Construction, Building, or a related field. * Strong communication skills and in\-depth knowledge of construction practices, trades, and techniques. * A proven track record in securing profitable projects. * Excellent IT skills. * A full clean European driving licence and access to transport. * Strong Microsoft Office skills. * The ability to work well as part of a team. * The ability to meet deadlines. * Excellent numeracy and computer skills. * A professional presence, dress, and demeanour. **Salary:** Negotiable, and will include a suitable benefits package.

title: Junior UX/UI designer
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * **Shape high\-leverage product experiences.** Design exceptional UI and UX—from early concepts to polished delivery—using the best available tools to explore more directions, move faster, and raise the quality bar. Ensure every experience is intuitive, effective, and delightful. * **Design with AI.** Use AI agents to turn rough ideas into interactive prototypes, integrate them with the design system, iterate through natural\-language feedback, and generate production\-ready frontend code. * **Prototype to validate.** Move fast between lo\-fi exploration and production\-realistic prototypes to stress\-test interactions and accelerate what gets built. * **Co\-create with PMs and engineers from day one.** Define UX requirements early, stay in the build through QA and release, and make engineering\-aware design decisions. * **Measure and improve.** Track funnels, retention, and adoption. Use qualitative and quantitative signals to assess design effectiveness and sharpen what ships next. * **Find the highest\-leverage bets from the backlog.** Identify where product improvements can unlock business value, align stakeholders around them, and move fast to deliver. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. **Commitment \& contract** Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Junior Warehouse Operative – Louth
description: Mon–Fri \| 8am–5pm Looking to get into a warehouse role with a solid, growing company? We’re hiring a Junior Warehouse Operative for a busy Drogheda\-based business working in IT products and hardware. ### **What You’ll Be Doing:** * Picking \& packing orders * Receiving and unloading deliveries * Quality checking goods * Updating daily reports (Excel) * General warehouse upkeep ### **What We’re Looking For:** * Strong work ethic * Good attention to detail * Reliable and hands\-on * Comfortable in a fast\-paced environment **Job Category:** Warehouse **Job Type:** Permanent **Job Location:** Drogheda

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: MDCE BEOL Integration Engineer
description: **Job Details:** ================ **Job Description:** -------------------- Join Intel and build a better tomorrow. Manufacturing Development Customer Engineering (MDCE) is Intel's newest organization within Intel Foundry Technology Manufacturing (FTM). We bridge the critical gap between Technology Development (TD) and High Volume Manufacturing (HVM), advancing technology nodes from initial product qualification to high\-yield production across multiple products while enhancing technologies for our foundry customers. MDCE is also chartered to develop new technologies on mature node infrastructure to bring new solutions to new customers at reliable yield and performance and low cost, and this is where you are going to play a role. MDCE engineers in Ireland work within F34 at Leixlip, Ireland. F34 opened in 2023 and began high\-volume production of Intel 4 technology. This milestone also represents the deployment by Intel of extreme ultraviolet lithography in high\-volume manufacturing for the first time globally. Since 1989, Intel has invested more than €30 billion in Ireland creating the most advanced industrial campus in Europe, and Intel Ireland is home to Europe’s most advanced high scale semi\-conductor foundry fab. Key **responsibilities** : * Work with FEOL/BEOL Integration, Device, Defect Reduction and Yield Analysis team members to identify root cause of yield/performance issues and quickly implement corrective plans to meet committed production yield targets * Collaborate with Technology Development and Local Yield teams to import new technology to production fabs. * Own product maintenance, ensuring products are successfully introduced to the fab and run on target. Optimize targets for yield, device, performance. * Establish and implement workflows and procedures for continuous improvement programs to ensure quality and prevent yield excursions in line and sort processes * Analyze structured and unstructured data to deliver insights into process health and enable decision on improvement activities * Assess quality and reliability risks for manufacturing materials and process changes, ensuring adherence to Intel's standards. **Qualifications:** ------------------- **Minimum** Qualifications * PhD degree in Electrical Engineering, Physics, Chemistry, Materials Science. * Good verbal and written communication skills, able to present complex problems and solutions to a wide audience * Able to work effectively in a team with strong collaboration * Strong aptitude for data mining and statistical analysis, with the ability to extract actionable insights from complex datasets **Preferred** Qualifications * Experience in new semiconductor technology development and FEOL/BEOL process optimization * Experience in fabrication process engineering including: (lithography and advanced patterning, dry etch processes, wet cleaning technologies, diffusion processes, thin\-film deposition, and chemical mechanical planarization (CMP) * Demonstrated problem\-solving and decision\-making skills in high\-pressure, fast\-paced environments. * Proven ability to lead cross\-functional teams and collaborate with diverse internal and external stakeholders. * A passion for driving industry\-leading solutions that align with Intel's values and technical vision. Why Join Intel MDCE? Intel offers an exceptional opportunity to work with cutting\-edge technology in a collaborative and innovative environment. This position provides a unique platform to shape the future of Intel's semiconductor solutions while directly supporting the company's efforts to meet diverse industry needs and maintain leadership in high\-volume manufacturing. Join us in driving the next generation of semiconductor manufacturing excellence and advancing Intel's position as a global technology leader. **Job Type:** ------------- College Grad **Shift:** ---------- Shift 1 (Ireland) **Primary Location:** --------------------- Ireland, Leixlip **Additional Locations:** ------------------------- **Business group:** ------------------- Intel Foundry strives to make every facet of semiconductor manufacturing state\-of\-the\-art while delighting our customers \- from delivering cutting\-edge silicon process and packaging technology leadership for the AI era, enabling our customers to design leadership products, global manufacturing scale and supply chain, through the continuous yield improvements to advanced packaging all the way to final test and assembly. We ensure our foundry customers' products receive our utmost focus in terms of service, technology enablement and capacity commitments. Employees in the Foundry Technology Manufacturing are part of a worldwide factory network that designs, develops, manufactures, and assembly/test packages the compute devices to improve the lives of every person on Earth. **Posting Statement:** ---------------------- All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance. **Position of Trust** --------------------- N/A **Work Model for this Role** This role will be eligible for our hybrid work model which allows employees to split their time between working on\-site at their assigned Intel site and off\-site. \* Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change. *

title: MEP Engineer
description: **Overview** John Sisk \& Son have been building excellence as a family\-owned international construction company by offering trust, certainty and value to our clients whilst being at the heart of change. Reporting to the Senior MEP Engineer or MEP Manager, the MEP Engineer is responsible for supporting the design, coordination and delivery of mechanical, electrical and plumbing systems on projects. The role ensures compliance with company standards, client requirements and regulatory obligations while contributing to efficient, high\-quality and sustainable project outcomes. **Responsibilities** **Design and Technical Support** * Assist in the development and review of MEP designs, drawings and specifications * Coordinate MEP systems with other disciplines to ensure constructability and compliance * Support technical problem\-solving on site and in design offices * Ensure designs comply with relevant regulations, standards and client requirements **Project Support** * Provide technical guidance to junior team members where applicable * Assist in planning, sequencing and installation of MEP systems on site * Support commissioning, testing and handover of MEP installations **Stakeholder Engagement** * Liaise with clients, consultants and subcontractors under guidance * Attend project meetings, design reviews and technical workshops * Communicate effectively across multidisciplinary project teams **Continuous Improvement** * Identify opportunities to improve design efficiency, quality and sustainability * Promote use of digital tools, BIM and innovative MEP solutions * Contribute to lessons learned and best practice sharing across projects **Experience** **Essential** * Experience in mechanical, electrical and plumbing engineering for construction projects * Knowledge of MEP systems, standards and regulations * Strong analytical, problem\-solving and organisational skills * Good communication and teamwork abilities **Desirable** * Experience on Tier 1 contractor projects or complex multi\-disciplinary programmes * Knowledge of low\-carbon, energy\-efficient and sustainable MEP design * Familiarity with digital construction tools, BIM and lean delivery methodologies **Qualifications** **Qualifications \& Training** **Essential** * Degree or equivalent in mechanical, electrical, building services or related discipline * Membership or eligibility for a relevant professional body (e.g., CIBSE, IET, Engineers Ireland) **Desirable** * Chartered or progressing towards chartered status with a relevant professional body * Postgraduate qualification in building services, project management or sustainability * Training in BIM management, digital design or advanced MEP technologies **Additional Information** * Competitive salary * 26 days annual leave * Pension and benefits * Unlimited learning and development opportunities * Much more **Our People and Recruitment Commitment** At Sisk Group our people are at the heart of our success. We offer the chance to work with purpose, build a career with no limits and be part of a great team. All vacancies are managed directly by our Internal Recruitment Team. Unsolicited contact or speculative CVs from unapproved agencies will not be accepted and no introductory fees will apply. We are an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion or any other protected characteristic. Reasonable adjustments are available during the recruitment process. We know the confidence gap and imposter phenomenon can stop talented candidates applying. You do not need to meet every criterion – your skills and potential matter. Don’t hold back we want to hear from you. All recruitment materials comply with legal and regulatory requirements including the EU Pay Transparency Directive and the Equality Acts UK and Ireland.

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Mechanical Engineer
description: Mechanical Engineer, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Key Role Responsibilities** **Team Management:** * Collaborate with engineers, and construction managers to develop comprehensive project plans and ensure delivery on schedule. * Collaborate with any other consultants, subcontractors, supervisors, planners, estimators, quantity surveyors and the general workforce involved in the project. * Liaise with clients and other 3rd parties as required, such as insurance companies, vendors, etc. * Develop comprehensive scope\-of\-works (SoW), task information deliverables lists (TIDP), and responsibility matrices (R\&R/ RACI) for Mechanical disciplines. * Raise any design\-related risks to planning, commercial and management, and establish a course to minimise their impact. * Maintain accurate records of key design activities (e.g. changes, issues) as they arise and escalate to management as appropriate. * Assist the Central Engineering and site teams with proactive contributions to construction and team matters. * Attend design related meetings and provide continual input for optimisation. * Collaborate on new design issues/ changes/ research within the team. * Assist with new tenders and PCSA (pre\-construction services agreements) deliverables within the Business Unit. **Project Management \& Planning** * Oversee the construction process to ensure adherence to design specifications and schedules. * Assist in developing design delivery schedules along with the client, project manager, design team and consultants. * Assist with managing the deliverables and approvals processes of design packages to ensure full approval for procurement. * Check design programmes on projects are aligned to consultant resource and construction/ procurement requirements. * Conduct site visits and report progress and quality of work. * Correctly follow information control and flow standards e.g. for RFIs, use of ACC, etc. **Technical Engineering** * Ensure Mechanical technical compliance on all projects. * Ensure knowledge of client and country\-specific design standards. * Assess and mitigate potential risks and issues as they’re identified in design, or that could have potential impact on construction activities. * Review design proposals by others for suitability and constructability. * Offer proposals for added value (VE) and off\-site manufacturing (OSM). * Ensure material choices are aligned to project requirements, budget, and QEHS standards. * Prepare comprehensive handover packs to the site team for any packages under your responsibility. * Coordinate and assist with coordination of Mechanical disciplines and interfaces with CSA and Electrical. * Check plans, drawings, and quantities for accuracy of calculations. * Review, monitor, and interpret the contract design documents supplied by the client or architect. **Sustainability** * Integrate sustainable practices into design and construction processes. * Evaluate the environmental impact of projects and propose options for improvements. **Documentation** * Prepare detailed reports, and documentation for project approval and permits. * Maintain accurate records of project progress and changes. * Manage key registers throughout the project design period: + Procurement schedule. + TIDP/ MIDP. + RFI log. + Deviation tracker. + Lessons Learnt log. + Issues, interface \& scope gap trackers. + Design change tracker. + Value Engineering Register + OSM Register **Other** * Provide technical guidance and support to construction teams. * Review and submit technical submittals. * Assist with recruitment activities for the Central Engineering team as deemed suitable. * Undertake other engineering tasks or duties as required by the Engineering Manager and/ or Company. * The Company reserves the right to vary the scope and nature of the Employee’s normal duties to consider changes in the Company’s work patterns and practices and the requirements of its business. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Minimum 5 years post graduate experience in the Industrial, Pharmaceutical, Life Sciences or Data Centre sectors. * Demonstration of design, technical and construction knowledge is essential for this role. * Experience in leading meetings and coordinating the other team members. * Strong working knowledge of design and analysis software such as BIM, Revit. * Clean driving licence essential **Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.** Apply Now

title: Multimedia Content Creator
description: **About the Role** ------------------ At Thérapie Clinic, we’ve been enhancing confidence and empowering lives for over 20 years. With more than 85 clinics across the UK, Ireland, and the USA, we’re proud to be Europe’s No.1 provider of Aesthetic Treatments. Our mission is simple: to make advanced, effective treatments accessible to everyone. Enjoy a fulfilling and rewarding career with opportunities for growth, development, and long\-term success. Join us and Do Your Best Work at Thérapie! As we continue to scale rapidly, we are looking for a talented and experienced **Multimedia Content Creator** to join us. This role offers the opportunity to shape the future of our workforce. The role of **Multimedia Content Creator** will join the Marketing Team based in Dublin. As someone who lives and breathes creativity, understands social media trends, and has the skills to bring ideas to life through engaging video and photography content, you’ll be responsible for producing scroll\-stopping content, working closely with our design and performance teams to deliver high\-impact creative including Long format Video, Short Format Reels and Photography assets for Thérapie Clinic. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ * Plan, shoot, and edit video and photo content tailored for social media platforms and our websites. * Long\-form content that tells a deeper story. For example: Explaining complex treatment services, Immersive Brand Storytelling \& Testimonials, Educational \& Authority\-Building Content, Comprehensive Product Education \& Conversion or guiding viewers toward a decision. * Short Format Reels/videos: Hook\-led, visually engaging creative videos for social media. Knowledge and experience with social media trends and visual hooks vital. Must speak the language of reels. Edit and repurpose longer videos into shorter clips * Work closely and proactively with design and performance teams to align content with campaign goals, brand standards, and in\-clinic execution. * Video editing/ photo retouching * Collaborate with the design team to deliver polished, on\-brand and elevated UGC feel content. * Take ownership of content projects from concept to delivery * Proactively pitch new ideas and content formats * Create content that drives engagement, reach, and conversions across social platforms, delivering content aligned with performance marketing goals. * Stay up\-to\-date with trends and techniques in video and photography for social media. * Travel to various locations across Ireland and the UK. * Full, clean driving licence preferred **Required Qualifications and Skills:** * Proven experience in video production (filming, lighting, audio capture). * Expertise in **Adobe Premiere Pro** or **Final Cut Pro**. * Proficiency in **CapCut** for mobile and social media editing. * Experience in both short\-form (Reels/TikTok) and long\-form video editing. * Skilled in storytelling and storyboarding techniques. * Experience in video color grading. * Strong photography skills, including camera handling and lighting. * Basic photo editing/retouching proficiency in **Adobe Creative Suite** (Photoshop/Lightroom). **Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Haves)** * Experience with **Adobe After Effects** for motion graphics. * Experience creating graphics in **Canva**. **Qualifications and Skills** ----------------------------- * Ideal for a recent graduate with a strong portfolio * 1–3 years’ experience (including freelance, internships, or personal projects)os (experience with tools like Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar is a plus). * Strong understanding of short\-form, social\-first content (Reels, TikTok, Shorts) * Proven experience in storyboarding, scripting, filming, and editing video content. * Demonstrable experience in photography. * Comfortable shooting both high\-end and mobile\-first content * Brings creativity, energy, and adaptability to every project. * Is highly organised and comfortable working on multiple projects at once. * What matters most is your skill, eye for detail, and passion for content creation. **Over to the best part, the Perks!** ------------------------------------- * #### **Uncapped Earnings** With our uncapped commission structure, the sky's the limit! * #### **Treat Yourself** Enjoy free laser hair removal, Free EmSella and generous discounts on our skin, AWI and body treatments – because our skin maestros deserve the best. * #### **Share the Love** Avail spouse and family discounts, spreading the joy of Thérapie. * #### **Sisterly Love** 10% off Thérapie Fertility \& Optilase treatments – because family helps family. * #### **Thérapie Fertility** We offer one free treatment cycle of IVF/ICSI/RIVF to all team members after they have been with us for 2 years. * #### **Optilase Eye Clinic** We offer free laser eye surgery to all team members after they have been with us for 2 years. * #### **Skin Royalty** Discounts on leading skin brands \& treatments (Murad, Skin Theory). * #### **Career Pathways** Embark on a journey of career progression \& management training – we believe in promoting from within! * #### **Knowledge Oasis** Immerse yourself in a full week of paid training in our academy, where knowledge is power. * #### **Big Birthday Bash Leave** Enjoy an extra day off on your landmark birthdays to celebrate in style! * #### **Longevity Bonus** Earn extra annual leave days after 2 years of dedicated service. * #### **Employee Assistance Scheme** Access confidential support services for personal and professional well\-being. **Application Process** ----------------------- STEP 1 **Explore Opportunities****:** Start by finding the role that excites you. STEP 2 **Submit Your Application****:** Apply by uploading your CV. STEP 3 **Application Review****:** Our Talent Acquisition Partners will carefully review your application. STEP 4 **Initial Contact****:** If shortlisted, our Talent Acquisition Team will reach out to schedule an interview. STEP 5 **Interview Stage****:** Complete the interview process with our team. STEP 6 **Offer \& Onboarding****:** If successful, you'll receive your offer along with onboarding details. STEP 7 **Begin Your Journey****:** Attend our state\-of\-the\-art training facility to kickstart your career with us.

title: Multimedia Content Creator
description: **CardHeading: \[As we continue to scale rapidly, we are looking for a talented and experienced **Multimedia Content Creator** to join us. ]** **CardIntro: \[As someone who lives and breathes creativity, understands social media trends, and has the skills to bring ideas to life through engaging video and photography content, you’ll be responsible for producing scroll\-stopping content, working closely with our design and performance teams to deliver high\-impact creative including Long format Video, Short Format Reels and Photography assets for Thérapie Clinic.]** **1\# About the Role** At Thérapie Clinic, we’ve been enhancing confidence and empowering lives for over 20 years. With more than 85 clinics across the UK, Ireland, and the USA, we’re proud to be Europe’s No.1 provider of Aesthetic Treatments. Our mission is simple: to make advanced, effective treatments accessible to everyone. Enjoy a fulfilling and rewarding career with opportunities for growth, development, and long\-term success. Join us and Do Your Best Work at Thérapie! As we continue to scale rapidly, we are looking for a talented and experienced **Multimedia Content Creator** to join us. This role offers the opportunity to shape the future of our workforce. The role of **Multimedia Content Creator** will join the Marketing Team based in Dublin. As someone who lives and breathes creativity, understands social media trends, and has the skills to bring ideas to life through engaging video and photography content, you’ll be responsible for producing scroll\-stopping content, working closely with our design and performance teams to deliver high\-impact creative including Long format Video, Short Format Reels and Photography assets for Thérapie Clinic. **3\# Key Responsibilities** * Plan, shoot, and edit video and photo content tailored for social media platforms and our websites. * Long\-form content that tells a deeper story. For example: Explaining complex treatment services, Immersive Brand Storytelling \& Testimonials, Educational \& Authority\-Building Content, Comprehensive Product Education \& Conversion or guiding viewers toward a decision. * Short Format Reels/videos: Hook\-led, visually engaging creative videos for social media. Knowledge and experience with social media trends and visual hooks vital. Must speak the language of reels. Edit and repurpose longer videos into shorter clips * Work closely and proactively with design and performance teams to align content with campaign goals, brand standards, and in\-clinic execution. * Video editing/ photo retouching * Collaborate with the design team to deliver polished, on\-brand and elevated UGC feel content. * Take ownership of content projects from concept to delivery * Proactively pitch new ideas and content formats * Create content that drives engagement, reach, and conversions across social platforms, delivering content aligned with performance marketing goals. * Stay up\-to\-date with trends and techniques in video and photography for social media. * Travel to various locations across Ireland and the UK. * Full, clean driving licence preferred **Required Qualifications and Skills:** * Proven experience in video production (filming, lighting, audio capture). * Expertise in Adobe Premiere Pro or Final Cut Pro. * Proficiency in CapCut for mobile and social media editing. * Experience in both short\-form (Reels/TikTok) and long\-form video editing. * Skilled in storytelling and storyboarding techniques. * Experience in video color grading. * Strong photography skills, including camera handling and lighting. * Basic photo editing/retouching proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop/Lightroom). **Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Haves)** * Experience with Adobe After Effects for motion graphics. * Experience creating graphics in Canva. **4\# Qualifications and Skills** * Ideal for a recent graduate with a strong portfolio * 1–3 years’ experience (including freelance, internships, or personal projects)os (experience with tools like Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar is a plus). * Strong understanding of short\-form, social\-first content (Reels, TikTok, Shorts) * Proven experience in storyboarding, scripting, filming, and editing video content. * Demonstrable experience in photography. * Comfortable shooting both high\-end and mobile\-first content * Brings creativity, energy, and adaptability to every project. * Is highly organised and comfortable working on multiple projects at once. * What matters most is your skill, eye for detail, and passion for content creation.

title: Operations Executive - Graduate Role
description: Are you a people person with good attention to detail? **Job description:** Atlas Travel Solutions is an Inbound Tour Operator/DMC with offices in London, Dublin, Florida, Monaco and Edinburgh. We are looking for a graduate student to work with our operations team providing ground handling services for groups travelling throughout Ireland. There will be full supervision and training given. This role is office based on Camden Street, Monday to Friday, with flexible hours if needed. Ideal hours are 9:00am to 5:30pm. Key Skills Required: * Fluency in Italian, French, Spanish, Scandinavian or another European language would be useful **although not mandatory.** * Good knowledge of Ireland as a destination and its tourism products * Ability to work as part of a team and take instruction * Computer skills including Microsoft applications particularly word, excel and powerpoint. * The ability to multi\-task, be level\-headed and full of enthusiasm * Excellent written and spoken English We will provide a complete breakdown of the role together with salary, etc at the interview stage. Preference is for someone who will be graduating with a tourism, events or language degree. This role has the opportunity to become a permanent position for the right candidate. Full training will be given in our operations system – Tourplan. We are flexible on start date and this role could be continued part time during term time. Preference would be given to anyone who is looking for a long term career in tourism after they graduate. ONLY APPLY IF YOU ARE A CURRENT OR ABOUT TO GRADUATE STUDENT AND IF YOU LIVE OR WILL BE LIVING IN EASY REACH OF DUBLIN THIS SUMMER. Please contact us with a copy of your CV and a cover letter as to why you are suitable for the role. If you have not been contacted within one week of application. Job Type: Temporary – but can become permanent during the summer for the right candidate. Contract length: 3 \- 6 months Work Location: In person Job Type: Full\-time Application question(s): * Where are you based in Ireland? Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Operations Risk & Resilience Placement - 2026 Work Placement
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin and Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. The Operations Consulting Team Supports Clients Across The Full Asset Lifecycle—from Acquisition And Planning Through To Operation And Disposal—by Addressing The Interdependencies Between People, Processes, Systems, And Assets. The Discipline Delivers a Broad And Integrated Set Of Services, Including * Operational Strategy \& Transformation: Helping clients align operational execution with strategic goals, including digital transformation initiatives and process improvement * Governance, Risk \& Compliance (GRC): Support the delivery of effective compliance and risk management programmes to identify, manage and mitigate business and digital related risk * Operational Readiness \& Performance Improvement: Ensuring systems, teams, and processes are prepared for launch or transition, and continuously optimised for performance * Digital Enablement: Leveraging tools like BIM, Digital Twins, and analytics platforms to improve operational visibility and decision\-making **The Opportunity** Arup Ireland are seeking talented students to join our Cork or Dublin team. We look for students who are ready to jump into the professional world; diverse individuals with inquisitive minds and a desire to challenge. You will work alongside skilled professionals and contribute to the success of real projects. You will also enjoy learning sessions with technical experts within your team, working on local projects and all kinds of social events. You will be working with a team of Operations Consulting Services professionals from various offices. The Operations Consulting team embodies Arup’s Total Design philosophy by integrating people, processes, technology, and physical assets into cohesive operational strategies that are embedded from concept through to implementation. The team works with a variety of public sector organisations, including Transport Infrastructure Ireland. With reference to our Risk \& Resilience service within Operations Consulting, it exemplifies Arup’s Total Design by embedding resilient governance frameworks, proactive risk intelligence, and compliance assurance into the operational phase of projects—ensuring that systems, processes, and people are holistically integrated. This is an office\-based role, with successful candidates expected to work 5 days per week in the Arup office in Cork or Dublin. **As a work placement student in the Operations team at Arup, you will:** * Support turning technical recommendations into operational plans and project deliverables with defined sustainability outcomes for our clients * Practice creative applications of technology to develop design solutions * Develop communication skills, including report writing and visual representations * Attend and participate in meetings – listen, ask questions * Be involved in discussions with team members to gain knowledge \& experience * Undertake analytical research to develop, document, present concepts and design ideas * Review project documentation, applicable codes and standards to assist with preparation of reports, presentations, drawings and/or sketches * Give structure to ideas – lots of “taking a first pass” at spreadsheets, reports and analyses for discussion later rather than instruction **Does this role inspire you?** We're Looking For Students Who Are Currently Studying For a Relevant Bachelor’s Degree Level Or Be Currently Undertaking a Post\-graduate Level Qualification In a Relevant Discipline, Including, But Not Limited To * Governance \& Compliance Management * Business in Operations Management * Management (Operations \& Supply Chain) * Supply Chain Operations * Business Information Systems * Computer Systems Engineering * Cybersecurity **We Also Value** * Experience of producing technical presentations and reports, knowledge sharing with peers * History of academic research, technical presentations, and technical report writing * Experience with any of the following: GRC software solutions, Cybersecurity solutions, Microsoft Visio, PowerPoint, or Project, or analysis packages * Knowledge of ISO Assurance Frameworks such as 27001 or 55001 and Data Privacy regulations * Evidence of teamwork and leadership in academic or extracurricular field * The ability to prioritise effectively, balance assigned work and deliver to deadlines **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Kickstart your career with our structured program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please visit our Candidate Support page for contact information and assistance. We're happy to help. **Please note this role is only suitable for those who are in full time education and are looking for a placement for the summer months or for those who are required to complete a placement as part of their course requirements.** **If you have recently graduated, please apply to one of our '2026 Graduate Programme' roles.** \#IREIntern **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Orthoptist
description: **Location** Portlaoise, Laois **Expire Date** 2026\-05\-15 **Salary** €50 \- €55 per hour **Job Ref** ORTHO85392\_1776439265 **Job Title** Senior Orthoptist **Location** Midlands (Mullingar, Laoise \& Athlone) **Speciality** Ophthalmology / Orthoptic Services Emerald Locums are currently recruiting for a Senior Orthoptist to join a well\-established ophthalmic service in the Midlands, covering sites across Mullingar, Laoise, and Athlone. This is an excellent opportunity to work within a dynamic acute and community\-integrated setting, supporting both adult and paediatric patients. This role offers exposure to a broad range of ophthalmic and orthoptic services, working as part of a multidisciplinary team across multiple clinical sites. Both permanent and long\-term contract opportunities may be available, with a strong focus on service development, clinical governance, and patient\-centred care. **What’s On Offer** * Competitive salary aligned with HSE pay scales * Opportunity to work across multiple clinical sites * Supportive multidisciplinary team environment * Ongoing professional development and training * Exposure to both adult and paediatric caseloads **Duties** * Provide comprehensive orthoptic assessments and treatments * Manage and prioritise a varied caseload independently and within a team * Work closely with ophthalmology consultants and wider MDT * Participate in clinical audits and service improvement initiatives * Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date clinical records * Support the supervision and training of junior staff and students **Requirements** * BSc (Hons) in Orthoptics or equivalent recognised qualification * Minimum of 3 years’ post\-qualification clinical experience * Registration with HCPC or eligibility for registration * Strong clinical, organisational, and communication skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team If you meet the above criteria and are interested, please apply with your up\-to\-date CV. INDHP * Portlaoise, Laois

title: PHD Graduate - Data Scientist
description: Purpose of the Position/ Role Summary We are offering an exciting opportunity to work in a rapidly growing global pharmaceutical organisation within the animal health sector. As a PHD Graduate Data Scientist, you will join the Bimeda IT Global Data \& Analytics team, contributing to impactful, data\-driven and AI\-driven initiatives across the business. This 24\-month program is designed to accelerate your development by giving you hands\-on experience with real\-world data and artificial intelligence challenges, exposure to global stakeholders, and the opportunity to deliver meaningful insights and intelligent solutions that support innovation and operational excellence. Role Title: PHD Graduate \- Data Scientist Reporting to: Head of Data Analytics Duration of Post: Fixed Term – 24 Months Contract Type: Full\-time Salary: €62,000\.00 Location: 21 Spruce Avenue, Stillorgan Industrial Park Key Role Specific Requirements * Analyse complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights * Develop and deploy predictive models, machine learning, and AI\-driven solutions * Contribute to the design, development, and implementation of AI initiatives across the global Bimeda organisation (e.g., automation, advanced analytics, generative AI use cases) * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to understand business requirements and translate them into data science and AI solutions * Support the development of data pipelines, AI models, dashboards, and reporting tools * Assist in evaluating and implementing emerging AI technologies and best practices * Contribute to data governance, model governance, and responsible AI practices * Present findings and recommendations to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Continuously explore new tools, techniques, and methodologies in data science and artificial intelligence Please note that this job description is not exhaustive, and the role holder may be required to undertake other relevant duties commensurate with the grading of the post. Activities may be subject to amendment over time as the role develops and/or priorities and requirements evolve What We Offer * An exciting opportunity to work in a rapidly growing global pharmaceutical organisation * Access to the latest software and modern data and AI platforms * A chance to work alongside experienced colleagues in data science, AI, IT, and the pharmaceutical industry * Exposure to global projects, cutting\-edge AI initiatives, and diverse datasets * Structured learning and development opportunities in both data science and AI * Mentorship from senior data and AI professionals * A collaborative and innovative working environment Why Join Us? You will be part of a global organisation making a real difference in animal health, while building a strong foundation in both data science and artificial intelligence. This role offers a unique blend of technical challenge, innovation, and real\-world impact **About You** * PHD graduate with a degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Experience with programming languages such as Python, R, or similar * Familiarity with data visualisation tools (e.g., Power BI) * Basic understanding of machine learning and AI concepts (e.g., NLP, predictive modelling) * Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly * A proactive mindset and eagerness to learn Desirable Skills * Exposure to AI/ML frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit\-learn) * Experience with large datasets and cloud platforms (e.g., Azure, AWS, GCP) * Knowledge of SQL and database management * Familiarity with generative AI tools or concepts (e.g., LLMs, prompt engineering) * Exposure to version control tools (e.g., Git) * Interest in the pharmaceutical or animal health industry

title: Part-Time  Sale Associate, Tommy Hilfiger Blanchardstown
description: **Be part of an iconic story.** TOMMY HILFIGER is one of the world’s most recognized global lifestyle brands, confidently welcoming and inspiring consumers since 1985\. Originally established in New York City and infused with the vibrant spirit of Americana, to create the modern uniform of accessible luxury. The TOMMY HILFIGER brands consist of TOMMY HILFIGER and TOMMY JEANS, complemented by a range of licensed product categories that extend the brand lifestyle across generations, geographies, activities and occasions. Tommy Hilfiger’s global marketing approach and communications strategy taps into culture through the world of F.A.M.E.S.: the people, organizations and ideas boldly driving the future of Fashion, Art, Music, Entertainment and Sport. Major global campaigns, sponsorship platforms, and disruptive activations with world\-class athletes, musicians and entertainers bring a constant source of energy and inspiration to the brand. Immerse yourself in TOMMY HILFIGER here! (YouTube, Instagram, TikTok) *****About* PVH**** With a history going back over 130 years, PVH has excelled at growing brands and businesses with rich American heritages, becoming one of the largest global apparel companies. We have over 30,000 associates operating in over 40 countries with over $8 billion in revenues. Founded in 1968 and acquired by PVH Corp. in 2003, Calvin Klein is a global lifestyle brand that exemplifies bold, progressive ideals and a seductive, and often minimal, aesthetic. We seek to thrill and inspire our audience while using provocative imagery and striking designs to ignite the senses. We strive to make a positive impact on the world. We inspire passion in our people. We embody authenticity and embrace individuality. Tommy Hilfiger is one of the world’s leading designer lifestyle brands, internationally recognized for celebrating the essence of “classic American cool” style. Founded in 1985, Tommy Hilfiger was acquired by PVH Corp. in 2010\. We engage consumers through 360° marketing campaigns, innovative events, exciting collaborations and brand partnerships, and work with some of the biggest names from the world of Fashion, Art, Music and Entertainment. *****About* THE ROLE**** Our stores are the life and soul of our business. They act as our main touch points with our ever\-evolving consumer base. As the world of retail evolves, we, as a business, believe that for all other parts of the chain to work, our stores must set the tone of what our two brands are all about. Irrespective of where our consumers eventually make a purchase, our stores, are always a window to our great brands. As a result, our locations are paramount, first\-class experience must be a given and our in\-store standards should never be questionable. To deliver this, we strive to hire the individuals who live and breathe the values of our business; who are obsessed with delivering great service to our customers and who truly believe that to remain relevant in today’s world, we must be nimble and adapt to change and evolution. The Sales Associate plays a key role in achieving these high standards ****Responsibilities Include:**** * Ensuring high levels of customer satisfaction through excellent service * First point of contact for authorizing discounts and resolving customer queries * Being a brand ambassador, demonstrating in\-depth product knowledge. * Building and maintaining professional relationships with our customers. * Outfit building for customers and making further product recommendations. * Assessing customers’ needs and providing assistance and information on product features. * Driving store KPIs and suggesting ways to improve. * Ensuring shop and stock room maintenance, presentation and organization issues are addressed in an appropriate manner. *****About* YOU**** * You'll connect to consumers and have a previous track record within hospitality or retail. * You'll be an effective communicator with the collaborate to win. * You'll inspire trust and recognize and celebrate the contributions and achievements of others. * You’ll adapt fast. * You’ll act with purpose, showing a clear presence on the shop floor. * You'll take ownership and make informed decisions to find in\-store solutions. *****About* WHAT WE OFFER**** At PVH, we understand that the success of our organization is directly related to our hardworking and dedicated associates who contribute their time and talents to help make our Company the success it is. That is why we are committed to providing a competitive and comprehensive benefits program that offers the protection, peace of mind and flexibility designed to support our associates – both at home and at work. PVH Europe and our brands, TOMMY HILFIGER and CALVIN KLEIN, are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse community of associates with a strong sense of belonging. We believe we are bettered by all forms of diversity and take pride in working with top talent from every walk of life and corner of the world. In the spirit of inclusivity, qualified applicants will be considered without regard to age, ethnicity, ability, gender, gender expression, gender identity, nationality, race, religion or sexual orientation. ****About PVH:**** We are brand builders who focus our passion and creativity to build Calvin Klein and TOMMY HILFIGER into the most desirable lifestyle brands in the world and at the same time position PVH as one of the best\-performing brand groups in our sector. Guided by our values and enabled by our scale and global reach, we are driving fashion forward for good, as one team with one vision and one plan. That’s the Power of Us, that’s the Power of PVH\+. One of PVH’s greatest strengths is our people. Our collective desire is to create a workplace environment where every individual is valued, and every voice is heard, and we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse community of associates with a strong sense of belonging. Learn more about Inclusion \& Diversity at PVH **here** . *PVH Corp. or its subsidiary ("PVH") is an equal opportunity employer and considers all applicants for employment on the basis of their individual capabilities and qualifications without regard to race, ethnicity, color, sex, gender identity or expression, age, religion, national origin, citizenship status, sexual orientation, genetic information, physical or mental disability, military status or any other characteristic protected under federal, state or local law. In addition to complying with all applicable laws, PVH is also committed to ensuring that all current and future PVH associates are compensated solely on job\-related factors such as skill, ability, educational background, work quality, experience and potential.*

title: Payments Analyst
description: At CurrencyFair, we're passionate about revolutionising the way people send and receive money around the world. As a peer\-to\-peer currency exchange, we're making it easier and more affordable for individuals and businesses to transfer funds across borders. We're looking for talented individuals who share our vision and want to join us in disrupting the traditional financial services industry. If you're excited about the prospect of using your skills to help people save money and time, we'd love to hear from you. Operating within the global support function of our Payments Team, the Payments Analyst is responsible for ensuring timely, seamless, well\-controlled and appropriately monitored end\-to\-end support of customer transaction flows, delivered in accordance with regulatory obligations, guidelines, associated rules and the organisation’s internal policies. **Key Responsibilities** * Ensure customers’ end\-to\-end transaction flows (i.e. from sending in through to transferring out) are processed accurately, and efficiently in accordance with the organisation’s and market opening times * Reconciliation of incoming payments, outgoing transfers and investigation of unreconciled items \- manually process all transactions that fall out of automated clearing / batching process, identify trends in automation failures and raise solutions to address and improve automated processing * Investigate all unreconciled items within agreed SLA * Process internal and external payment instructions via various banking platforms * Communicate effectively by email and/or telephone with customers, sellers/banking partners other internal departments * Ensure work is carried out and completed in a controlled, compliant, timely and accurate manner with emphasis on attention to detail ensuring team SLAs and KPIs are met * Execute foreign exchange transactions * Carry out daily liquidity management across different banking partners * Support and work with external service providers such as banking and non\-bank partners * Support Product and Engineering teams with testing of new product offerings when required Open and close FX markets when required **Subject Matter Knowledge And Experience** * Experience of working in a financial services environment essential, with specific focus on reconciliation/cash/payments/FX operations * Proficient in MS Excel, with experience of using for financial reconciliations * Ability to maintain accuracy and attention to detail while meeting tight processing deadlines * Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, with ability to deal with all levels in a confident and professional manner Currencyfair is committed to diversity and inclusion. By providing equal opportunities we foster a work environment which embraces diversity and gets the best out of the broadest spectrum of people to sustain business performance and competitive advantage. We build an inclusive culture by demonstrating respect for each other’s unique strengths and perspectives to enable every employee to develop a sense of belonging and have the opportunity to maximise their potential.

title: Physics Researcher - Fully Remote
description: **About The Job** **Mercor** connects elite creative and technical talent with leading AI research labs. Headquartered in San Francisco, our investors include **Benchmark** , **General Catalyst** , **Peter Thiel** , **Adam D'Angelo** , **Larry Summers** , and **Jack Dorsey** . **Position:** Physics Subject Matter Expert **Type:** **Contract** **Compensation:** **$85/hour** **Location:** **Remote** **Commitment:** **4–6 tasks/week** **Role Responsibilities** * Develop challenging problems in Physics to inform future AI innovations. * Create high\-quality data sets for AI model training and evaluation. * Collaborate with AI research teams to enhance model outputs. * Work independently and asynchronously to meet deadlines. * Leverage expertise in Statistical Mechanics, Condensed Matter, and AMO Physics. **Qualifications** **Must\-Have** * PhD in Physics with specialization in Advanced Quantum Mechanics, Advanced Electrodynamics, or Advanced Classical Mechanics. * Graduate degree from US/UK/Canada/Western Europe. * High attention to detail. * Exceptional written and verbal communication skills. * Excellent proficiency in English. **Start Date** * February; applications reviewed on a rolling basis. **Application Process (Takes 20–30 mins to complete)** * Upload resume * AI interview based on your resume * Submit form **Resources \& Support** * For details about the interview process and platform information, please check: https://talent.docs.mercor.com/welcome/welcome * For any help or support, reach out to: support@mercor.com *PS: Our team reviews applications daily. Please complete your AI interview and application steps to be considered for this opportunity.*

title: Platform Owner - Public Cloud
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Do as a Platform Owner – Public Cloud** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Lead and scale Cloud Engineering \& Operations, partnering with strategic technology vendors to deliver secure, self‑service cloud platforms that accelerate product team delivery and customer value. * Establish robust cloud governance and standards, ensuring excellence across cost management, security operations, deployment practices, compliance, and policy adherence. * Drive operational performance and service quality, defining metrics and controls to improve availability, workload utilisation, SLA performance, service transition rigour, and security posture. * Provide hands‑on technical leadership, setting cloud strategy and architecture direction, resolving complex escalations, and communicating clearly with senior stakeholders. * Build and develop high‑performing engineering teams, including line management, mentoring, talent strategy execution, and fostering accountability through servant leadership. * Support cloud transformation and strategic alignment, ensuring roadmaps, backlogs, and operating models align with business objectives while strengthening senior stakeholder and partner relationships. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Proven cloud leadership experience (8\+ years) leading Cloud Engineering and/or SRE teams, with a strong engineering background across enterprise‑scale systems, platforms, and operational environments. * Deep expertise in Microsoft Azure, delivering secure, scalable, and fault‑tolerant architectures using PaaS, serverless, containerisation, and Infrastructure‑as‑Code (Terraform preferred), with working knowledge of GCP as an advantage. * Strong operational and financial acumen, including cloud cost management, budgeting, forecasting, workload optimisation, and governance across security operations, compliance, and policy enforcement. * Advanced DevOps, CI/CD and observability capabilities, with hands‑on experience in logging, monitoring, reliability engineering, enterprise‑grade deployments, and advocating modern Agile, Lean, and DevOps practices. * Visionary and forward‑thinking technologist, able to identify emerging technologies, translate them into business value, inspire new ways of working, and successfully lead transformation initiatives. * Exceptional people and partner leadership skills, including managing and influencing third‑party providers, mentoring high‑performing engineering communities, and excelling in stakeholder management, problem solving, and multi‑stream delivery. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** JR\-4307

title: Port Heritage and Special Projects Intern
description: **About Us** Dublin Port Company is a State\-owned commercial entity responsible for the operation and development of Dublin Port, Ireland’s busiest port, handling almost 50% of national trade. Located at the heart of Dublin City, the port is a vital piece of national infrastructure and is supported by significant long\-term investment. The Port Heritage and Special Projects function supports Dublin Port Company’s commitment to preserving and sharing the port’s rich history. Central to this work is the Dublin Port Archive, which holds over 300 years of records, photographs, drawings, and artefacts and provides public access to this important cultural and historical resource in line with statutory obligations. **The Role** Dublin Port Company is seeking a Port Heritage and Special Projects Intern to join the team as part of the 2026 internship programme. This is a 10‑week paid internship, commencing in mid‑June 2026, offering hands\-on experience in archival preservation, collections management, research, and public engagement. Working under expert guidance, the intern will support the cataloguing, documentation, and care of Dublin Port’s heritage collections, with a strong focus on the photographic archive. The role also includes contributing to research and digital content, assisting with heritage interpretation projects, and supporting exhibitions and cultural activities delivered as part of the summer programme. This position provides practical exposure to archive and heritage operations within a nationally significant organisation. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with the cataloguing, organisation, and preservation of the photographic collection and other archival materials. * Support the transfer and documentation of archive materials from operational facilities to the Dublin Port Archive. * Maintain accurate records and inventories using Excel and other documentation tools. * Ensure appropriate handling, packaging, and rehousing of heritage materials in line with best practice, including participation in a lantern slide preservation project. * Conduct research to support blog content and interpretive material for the Dublin Port Archive website. * Assist with indexing historic registers and documents to support the development of a searchable digital archive. * Support research and interpretation of heritage artefacts at the Flour Mill premises. * Assist with the delivery of exhibitions, cultural projects, and public engagement activities. * Provide general support to the Port Heritage and Special Projects team as required. **Qualifications And Experience** * Currently enrolled in a relevant undergraduate programme (e.g. history, heritage studies, archives, museum studies, library science, cultural studies, archaeology, art history, or anthropology). * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team. * Basic knowledge of Microsoft Excel. * An interest in archives, heritage, or collections management. **How To Apply** Applicants must submit a CV and a short cover letter (maximum 500 words). Given that this role is intended for undergraduate students, the cover letter will be used to assess motivation, interest in heritage and archives, and relevant transferable skills, rather than relying on prior experience alone. **Our Commitment to Diversity** Dublin Port Company values diversity and is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace. Applications are welcomed from candidates of all backgrounds.

title: Preconstruction Planner
description: **\*\*HYBRID\*\*** **Collen** is recruiting a **Preconstruction Planner** to support our bid and early delivery functions. This role is ideal for ambitious junior planners, or experienced site engineers/managers who want to move into planning and shape how major projects are delivered from the very beginning. You will work within a high\-performing preconstruction team, contributing to programmes on large\-scale data centres, pharma facilities, power infrastructure, public works, and complex commercial projects. You will learn how delivery strategy is built at the preconstruction stage. **Who We’re Looking For** You’ll fit this role if at least one of the below applies: **1\. Junior / Intermediate Planner** * Experience with Primavera P6 or Microsoft Project (formal or self\-taught) * Some involvement in programming, commissioning plans, lookaheads, or tender support * Curious, organised, and good at translating drawings into sequence and logic **2\. Site Engineer, MEP Coordinator or Site Manager Ready for Change** * Hands\-on delivery experience — you understand how jobs are actually built * Familiar with programmes, sequencing, subcontractor coordination, logistics and project workflow * Keen to step off the tools/site and into early\-stage project strategy and planning * Strong communicator who can explain project realities confidently * We value real construction experience as much as software knowledge **Ideal Skills \& Attributes** * Solid grasp of construction phasing, access, resourcing, or commissioning logic * Basic ability to work with programmes or planning tools — or willingness to learn fast * Comfortable asking questions, challenging unrealistic assumptions, and learning from senior planners * Able to interpret drawings, method statements, or tender documents logically * Calm, organised, and capable of working in a deadline\-driven environment For detailed job description please visit our website here.
